title: KRAS Drug Cracks Pancreatic Cancer - TCR 05/09/26
description:
A Dana-Farber team reported in the New England Journal of Medicine that a new drug class targeting a family of mutant KRAS proteins extended survival in pancreatic cancer patients, Washington's Peninsula school district disclosed roughly $220,000 in annual savings from building its own ed-tech with Claude Code including a teacher-feedback platform called LessonLens, and Cloudflare cut 1,100 employees - 20% of headcount - on the same earnings call that posted record $639.8 million Q1 revenue up 34% year-over-year.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-9-2026/

0:00 Peninsula district builds its own ed-tech
0:35 KRAS-family drug cracks pancreatic cancer
7:07 Talking To The Bacteria
9:20 Cloudflare layoffs and Oracle severance asymmetry
13:49 Evergy gas commitment vs TSMC offshore wind

This episode covers the Dana-Farber NEJM KRAS-family drug trial against the forty-year "undruggable" verdict on pancreatic cancer, the Peninsula school district's AI-built ed-tech and the dissolving buyer-builder boundary, the University of Minnesota quorum-sensing periodontal therapy, the Cloudflare AI-attributed layoffs alongside Oracle's failed severance negotiation and Tom Steyer's California jobs guarantee proposal, and Evergy's 4.7 GW gas commitment against TSMC's gigawatt offshore wind PPA.

Brian Wolpin's team at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute reported in the New England Journal of Medicine a drug that blocks a family of mutant KRAS variants in pancreatic cancer patients whose tumors had progressed past standard care - reaching a target medicinal chemistry called "undruggable" for forty years. The same instrument layer underneath - cryo-EM, AI-assisted molecular design, fragment-based discovery, mass-spectrometry proteomics - is running beneath the Mayo Clinic pancreatic detector from April 30 and the Minnesota team's lactonase-based periodontal therapy that guides oral bacteria toward health-associated species without killing the community. Washington's Peninsula school district built LessonLens and a stack of operational tools with Claude Code, citing roughly $220,000 in annual savings and dissolving the boundary between ed-tech buyer and builder. Cloudflare disclosed 1,100 layoffs on a record-revenue earnings call attributing the cuts to AI productivity gains, while laid-off Oracle workers forfeited up to $1 million in unvested stock and Tom Steyer proposed a California jobs guarantee. And Evergy committed 4.7 GW of new gas through 2044 against TSMC's 30-year gigawatt offshore wind PPA - same compute demand, opposite forty-year bets.
